name: Tart Dough and Prebaking
recipeIngredient: 12 tablespoons cold butter cut into tablespoon pieces
6 tablespoons chilled shortening cut into tablespoon pieces
1/2 cup grated Gruyere or Swiss cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor combine the flour with the salt. Process until combined.
2. Add the cold butter and shortening; process until they are finely chopped.
3. Gradually add 8 tablespoons of cold water, processing until a ball forms. If too dry, add water tablespoon by tablespoon.
4. Turn dough onto a lightly floured board and press into a round slab about 1-inch thick.
5. If the dough is not too hot to handle, roll it out immediately (easier to manage). If fat oozes out, refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to relax the dough.
6. Gently flour your board and rolling pin. Roll dough from the center until a circle about 1/2-inch thick forms; then continue rolling until slightly thicker than 1/8-inch and 14 inches in diameter.
7. Transfer the rolled dough onto a lightly buttered, false-bottomed tart shell. Press into the pan, trimming edges to form a 1/2-inch wide border that folds back without overlapping the bottom.
8. Press the border into the sides of the tart shell to build up double thickness. Use a rolling pin to trim excess pastry and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
9. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place the pan on a baking sheet. Line the crust with foil or parchment, fill with dried beans, and bake for 10 minutes.
10. Remove beans, then bake an additional 10 minutes. Brush the bottom with beaten egg and sprinkle grated cheese; return to oven for 3-4 minutes to seal the base.
11. Allow tart shell to cool on a rack for 30 minutes before filling and finishing baking.
Yield: enough for two 8-inch tart shells or one 12-inch tart
